name: Orangenplaetzchen from Brigitte Sealing
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream butter and sugar in a mixer at high speed for 1 to 2 minutes until light and fluffy.
2. Add orange peel and salt; beat for an additional minute.
3. Beat in egg.
4. Gradually add chocolate and flour, mixing only until combined.
5. Divide dough into two equal parts. Flatten each part into a 6-inch circle, wrap in plastic wrap, and chill at least 2 hours or overnight.
6. Preheat oven to 375°F.
7. Roll out one circle of dough between sheets of lightly floured waxed paper until just slightly thicker than 1/4 inch. Sprinkle with flour once during rolling.
8. Cut into rounds using 2-1/2-inch round cutters, keeping dough on the bottom sheet of wax paper.
9. Slide dough onto a cookie sheet and freeze for 5 minutes.
10. Remove from freezer and transfer to a lightly greased baking sheet, spacing cookies about 1 inch apart.
11. Bake in the middle of the oven for 8-10 minutes or until lightly browned on edges.
12. Transfer immediately to wire racks to cool.
13. For glaze: Whisk powdered sugar with orange juice until smooth. Brush glaze onto warm cookies. Once glaze is set, store with wax paper between layers in airtight containers.
